overallAssessment: The agent assigned Tier-A to a child NFR that structurally belongs at Tier-C or Tier-D based on its description and AC count. The child names a specific quality sub-area (temporal bookending of audit windows) that is atomic and decomposable only along single measurable dimension — not a macro quality area warranting Tier-A classification. Additionally, a hint-override to Tier-A was made without adequate decomposition rationale. Per catalog §5.4.1: MEDIUM for within-one-tier misclassification, HIGH for cross-tier errors where the description clearly places the child in the atomic range.
